description: Fix three documentation inaccuracies: (1) the configuration example shows a plain string for a url-type setting but the server requires Record<string,string>, (2) the architecture Key Source Files section references a non-existent permissions.ts file, (3) the MCP server docs state the concurrent tool call limit is 5 but the actual limit is 25.
qualityChecks: cd docs && npm run build && npm run type-check && npm run lint && npm run knip && npm run format:check

userStories.title: Fix url-type settings example to show Record<string,string> format
userStories.description: The example config in reference/configuration.mdx shows a plain string for a url-type setting ("instanceUrl": "https://mycompany.atlassian.net"), but the settings resolver requires Record<string,string> (instance name → URL map). Update the example and surrounding prose to show the correct format.
acceptanceCriteria: The example config in reference/configuration.mdx no longer shows a plain string value for a url-type setting
The example shows a Record<string,string> format (instance name → URL) for the url-type setting, e.g. {"prod": "https://mycompany.atlassian.net"}
The prose description of url-type settings in the 'settings' field details section accurately reflects that the value is a map of instance names to URLs

userStories.title: Fix Key Source Files section to reference state.ts instead of non-existent permissions.ts
userStories.description: The contributing/architecture.mdx Key Source Files section lists `platform/mcp-server/src/permissions.ts` as a source file for permission evaluation, but this file does not exist. The permission evaluation function `getToolPermission` lives in `platform/mcp-server/src/state.ts`. Update the listing to reference state.ts.
acceptanceCriteria: The Key Source Files section in contributing/architecture.mdx no longer references `platform/mcp-server/src/permissions.ts`
The section references `platform/mcp-server/src/state.ts` for permission evaluation (getToolPermission) and server state management
userStories.notes:
Files:
- docs/content/docs/contributing/architecture.mdx (update Key Source Files section at the end)

Context:
The file `platform/mcp-server/src/permissions.ts` does NOT exist in the codebase. The Key Source Files section near the bottom of architecture.mdx currently lists:
```
- `platform/mcp-server/src/permissions.ts` — permission evaluation and resolution
```

The actual permission evaluation function `getToolPermission` is defined in `platform/mcp-server/src/state.ts` at line 445. The state.ts file is not currently listed in Key Source Files.

Changes:
In the Key Source Files section, replace:
```
- `platform/mcp-server/src/permissions.ts` — permission evaluation and resolution
```
with:
```
- `platform/mcp-server/src/state.ts` — in-memory server state, plugin registry, and permission evaluation (`getToolPermission`)
```

Also update `lastUpdated` frontmatter to `2026-04-15`.

Constraints:
- Do NOT change any other section of the architecture doc
- Do NOT add, remove, or reorder other files in the Key Source Files list
- Do NOT change any source code files

userStories.title: Fix concurrent tool call limit in mcp-server docs from 5 to 25
userStories.description: The Tool Dispatch section in reference/mcp-server.mdx states 'Up to 5 concurrent tool calls per plugin' but the actual limit defined in the source code is 25 (MAX_CONCURRENT_DISPATCHES_PER_PLUGIN = 25 in platform/mcp-server/src/mcp-tool-dispatch.ts line 36). Update the doc to reflect the correct limit.
acceptanceCriteria: The Tool Dispatch section in reference/mcp-server.mdx states 25 concurrent tool calls per plugin, not 5
userStories.notes:
Files:
- docs/content/docs/reference/mcp-server.mdx (update concurrent tool call limit in Tool Dispatch section)

Context:
The source of truth is `platform/mcp-server/src/mcp-tool-dispatch.ts` line 36:
```typescript
const MAX_CONCURRENT_DISPATCHES_PER_PLUGIN = 25;
```

The incorrect text is in the Tool Dispatch section (line 693 of mcp-server.mdx):
```
Timeouts: 30 seconds by default, extended by progress reports (up to 5 minutes maximum). Up to 5 concurrent tool calls per plugin; additional calls return an error asking the client to wait for in-flight requests to complete.
```

Changes:
Replace `Up to 5 concurrent tool calls per plugin` with `Up to 25 concurrent tool calls per plugin`.

Also update `lastUpdated` frontmatter to `2026-04-15`.

Constraints:
- Do NOT change any other text in the Tool Dispatch section
- Do NOT change any source code files
- This is a single number change (5 → 25) in one sentence
